name: Vincamine
description: Vincamine is a monoterpenoid indole alkaloid obtained from the leaves of *Vinca minor* with a vasodilatory property. Studies indicate that vincamine increases the regional cerebral blood flow.

interactions.name: Clarithromycin
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Vincamine can be increased when it is combined with Clarithromycin.

interactions.name: Erythromycin
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Vincamine can be increased when it is combined with Erythromycin.

interactions.name: Telithromycin
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Vincamine can be increased when it is combined with Telithromycin.

interactions.name: Solithromycin

interactions.name: Tylosin
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Vincamine can be increased when it is combined with Tylosin.

interactions.name: Acemetacin
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Vincamine can be decreased when used in combination with Acemetacin.

interactions.name: Amifostine
interactions.description: Vincamine may increase the hypotensive activities of Amifostine.

interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Posaconazole is combined with Vincamine.
